title: IPO chase and six-month price-action model
question: Do new listings pay off if you buy in the aftermarket, and can that base rate support a reusable six-month IPO scorecard?
finding: Broad aftermarket IPO chasing is a base-rate loser: 760 US IPOs since 2022-10 had a day-1-close one-year median excess return of -28.4% versus SPY, and only 19% beat the index. The same base-rate table now feeds the Study 15 six-month model: the broad 180-day prior is -15.4% versus SPY with only 25% beating SPY, then the scorecard adjusts for institutional IPO quality, offering mechanics, underwriters, holder/control risk, market setup, index flow, lock-up supply and business quality. SpaceX screens as a negative six-month bucket with a -20.2% midpoint, 22.4% probability of beating SPY and 18.4% probability of beating QQQ.
